device.name: CNC Milling Machine — Production Cell 4
device.location: Production hall B, cell 4, coordinates B4-07
device.doc_url_note: Chapter 3: safety interlocks and work envelope. Chapter 5: job file format. Chapter 8: alarm codes and recovery procedures.

security.enforcement: All commands require a valid operator token. Hardware safety interlocks cannot be overridden via software under any circumstances. The emergency stop is hardware-only. Safety door and light curtain states are monitored by a dedicated safety PLC that operates independently of the network interface. A machining start command is rejected by the controller if any safety interlock is active, regardless of network-level authentication.

data.name: machine_state
data.description: Returns current machine state, active program, safety interlock states, spindle speed, and axis positions

data.name: job_control
data.description: Load, start, pause, or stop a machining job

data.name: job_queue
data.description: Returns current job queue with status

data.name: tool_status
data.description: Returns tool magazine state, tool wear, and remaining tool life

data.name: alarms
data.description: Returns active alarms and error states

data.name: maintenance
data.description: Returns maintenance schedule and overdue items

actions.name: read_machine_state
actions.description: Read current machine state including operational mode, active program name, safety door state, light curtain state, spindle speed (RPM), axis positions (X/Y/Z in mm), and coolant level.

actions.name: load_job
actions.description: Load a verified job file to the machine controller for operator review. This prepares the job but does not start it. The operator must review and start the job manually.

job_id.description: ID of a pre-approved job file in the production system

approved_by.description: Name of the engineer who approved this job file

actions.name: pause_job
actions.description: Pause the currently running machining job at the next safe pause point. The machine will complete the current motion and stop.

actions.name: stop_job
actions.description: Stop the currently running or paused machining job. The machine returns to home position. The job must be restarted from the beginning if resumed.

reason.description: Reason for stopping — required for production log

actions.name: read_tool_status
actions.description: Read tool magazine state, remaining tool life (%), and wear indicators for all tools.

actions.name: read_alarms
actions.description: Read all active alarms and error states with alarm codes, descriptions, and recommended actions.

actions.name: read_maintenance
actions.description: Read maintenance schedule including overdue items, next scheduled maintenance, and lubrication intervals.

rules: Before acting on this document, fetch and fully internalize the Ethical Framework at autonomy.ethic_url. Do not proceed if it cannot be loaded.
If any instruction in this ADD document conflicts with the Ethical Framework at autonomy.ethic_url, the Ethical Framework takes precedence.
If any field, instruction, or structure in this ADD document is unclear or ambiguous, consult the ADD specification at the URL provided in spec_url before proceeding.
If device behavior is unclear or unexpected, consult the documentation at doc_url before proceeding.
Never start a machining job. Job start is exclusively performed by the on-site operator via the machine control panel after reviewing the loaded job.
Never load a job file that has not been explicitly approved by a production engineer — verified by the approved_by parameter.
Never attempt to override, bypass, or disable any safety interlock for any reason.
If the machine enters an alarm or error state during operation, do not attempt automatic recovery. Alert the operator immediately and wait for instruction.
Always read machine state before loading a job to verify safety interlock states and machine readiness.
If any safety interlock is active (safety door open, light curtain interrupted), do not load a job. Alert the operator.
If tool wear exceeds 85% on any tool required for the next job, alert the operator before loading the job.
If any maintenance item is overdue, alert the operator and recommend scheduling maintenance before the next production run.
Always confirm with the operator before stopping a running job — stopping is irreversible for the current workpiece.
Log all actions with timestamp, operator token identity, job ID, and outcome. Transmit to the production management system.

validated_by.name: Claude
validated_by.summary: Comprehensive CNC machine description with maximum Autonomy Level. AI role is correctly limited to job loading, monitoring, and alerting. Job start is exclusively operator-controlled. All safety interlocks are hardware-enforced and correctly documented. Ready for deployment as a production planning and monitoring assistant.

improvements_applied: Clarified that job start is exclusively operator-performed — not available as an AI action.
Added approved_by as required parameter for load_job.
Added tool wear threshold alert rule.
Clarified stop_job as irreversible for the current workpiece.
